name: decision
description: The verdict slide — one chosen path, named explicitly.
purpose: Use after a comparison slide to land the decision. The chosen option is the focal element; the rejected ones (if shown) are subordinated.

options.description: List items. Lead each with **Option name.** then a short rationale. The chosen option carries the focal styling.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: decision -->

## What we are doing.

- **Chosen path.** One-line rationale for the decision.
- **Rejected option.** One-line rationale for why this didn't fit.
sample:
<!-- _class: decision -->

## What we are doing.

- Chosen path.
  - Self-contained per-component folders at lib/components, one folder per component. Holds manifest plus styles plus optional transform plus example plus README.
- Rejected option.
  - Flat files alongside each other in lib/components. Defeats the self-contained goal and leaves transform.js scattered.

whenToUse.title: Land the verdict
whenToUse.body: Follows a comparison slide to make the chosen path unambiguous. The heading carries the verb of the decision; the cards substantiate it.

whenToUse.title: Two to four justifications
whenToUse.body: Each card is one short rationale — the chosen path first, then a Why-not card for each rejected alternative. More than four crowds the horizontal strip.

whenToUse.title: After the comparison
whenToUse.body: Pair with `compare-prose` or `split-compare` upstream — that slide does the weighing, this slide lands the answer. Reaching for decision without a prior comparison reads as edict.

antiPatterns.title: No clear chosen path
antiPatterns.body: If the cards don't name one focal verdict, the slide is back to being a comparison. Use `compare-prose` or `split-compare`; reserve decision for the resolved call.

antiPatterns.title: Long body per card
antiPatterns.body: Each card is one sentence of rationale. Paragraphs belong on the comparison slide upstream, not on the verdict slide.

antiPatterns.title: Generic heading
antiPatterns.body: The h2 carries the decision verb — Build, not buy. Adopt the framework. Pause the rollout. A heading like Next steps wastes the focal real estate.

related.name: compare-prose
related.when: the comparison slide that should precede decision

related.name: split-compare
related.when: comparison and verdict on one slide instead of two

related.name: closing
related.when: the deck ends without a single-call verdict

related.name: big-number
related.when: the decision is a quantitative commitment
